name: Art Institute of Chicago
description: A world-renowned museum housed in a landmark Beaux-Arts complex spanning nearly one million square feet across multiple levels. It features a vast collection of approximately 300,000 works, including iconic masterpieces such as Grant Wood's American Gothic, Edward Hopper's Nighthawks, and Georges Seurat's A Sunday on La Grande Jatte.

always_free_groups.category: Electronic Benefits Transfer and Women Infants and Children cardholders

name: Field Museum
address: 1400 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60605
description: A world-renowned natural history museum in Chicago, it occupies over one million square feet within a landmark Beaux-Arts complex. The institution houses nearly 40 million specimens and is most famous for SUE the T. rex, Maximo the Titanosaur, and its extensive collection of Egyptian mummies.

always_free_groups.category: Electronic Benefits Transfer or Women, Infants, and Children cardholders

name: Shedd Aquarium
address: 1200 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60605
description: A world-renowned National Historic Landmark housed in a Beaux-Arts marble building, this facility contains 5 million gallons of water across multiple levels. It features a vast collection of 32,000 animals, including iconic species such as beluga whales, Pacific white-sided dolphins, and a giant Pacific octopus.

always_free_groups.category: Chicago police officers and Chicago firefighters
Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, and Wisconsin educators
Museums in the Park employees and volunteers

name: Museum of Science & Industry
address: 5700 S DuSable Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60637
description: This premier science museum is housed in a stunning 400,000-square-foot Beaux-Arts structure that remains from the 1893 World's Fair. It features world-renowned interactive exhibits including a German U-505 submarine, a full-size replica coal mine, the Apollo 8 command module, and a 40-foot tornado.

always_free_groups.category: United States military active-duty personnel and veterans
Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) or Women, Infants and Children (WIC) cardholders

name: Adler Planetarium
address: 1300 S DuSable Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60605
description: As the first planetarium in the Western Hemisphere, this world-renowned institution features 60,000 square feet of exhibitions housed in a landmark Art Deco building. It is famous for its immersive domed theaters, the historic Atwood Sphere, and rare artifacts including the Gemini 12 spacecraft and a 1250 AD astrolabe.

always_free_groups.category: DuSable Black History Museum and Education Center members
National Museum of Mexican Art members
ASTC Travel Passport Program members and employees

name: Lincoln Park Zoo
description: One of the oldest and most historic zoos in North America, this 35-acre free institution features naturalistic habitats and four landmark buildings. It is home to nearly 1,100 animals representing 200 species, including notable inhabitants such as western lowland gorillas, African lions, polar bears, and Sichuan takins.

name: National Museum of Mexican Art
description: A prominent first-voice institution and the largest Latino cultural organization in the United States, located in Chicago's Pilsen neighborhood. It houses a permanent collection of over 22,000 works spanning 3,600 years, including Mesoamerican artifacts, Mexican folk art, and significant archives from artists like Carlos Cortéz.

name: Museum of Contemporary Art
description: One of the world's largest contemporary art venues, this four-story, 220,000-square-foot institution features a cast aluminum and limestone facade. Its multidisciplinary collection of over 2,500 works includes iconic pieces by Andy Warhol, Jasper Johns, and Alexander Calder, alongside a rotating schedule of performances and experimental exhibitions.

always_free_groups.category: Illinois elementary and high school teachers
Visitors with disabilities and their caregivers
Active members of the military, police, and fire departments
University of Chicago students, faculty, and staff

name: Chicago History Museum
description: As the city's oldest cultural institution, this major research center is housed in a Georgian colonial building in Lincoln Park and manages over 22 million artifacts. It features significant pieces like the bed where Abraham Lincoln died, the city's first locomotive, and remnants from the Great Chicago Fire.

always_free_groups.category: Students, faculty, and staff at City Colleges of Chicago, DePaul University, and University of Illinois Chicago
North American Reciprocal Museum Association Members

name: Garfield Park Conservatory
address: 300 N Central Park Ave, Chicago, IL 60624
description: A nationally renowned botanical treasure, this institution features 12 acres of indoor and outdoor gardens within a landmark structure designed to resemble a Midwestern haystack. It houses thousands of plant species across eight display rooms, most notably the historic Fern Room which contains prehistoric cycads and a naturalistic stone waterfall.

always_free_groups.category: Link and Women Infants and Children cardholders

name: Smart Museum of Art
description: A distinguished academic art museum located in a modernist limestone building, housing a diverse collection of over 17,000 objects. It is globally recognized for its interdisciplinary research and notable pieces, including original Frank Lloyd Wright furniture from the Robie House and sculptures by Auguste Rodin.

name: DuSable Black History Museum
description: The nation's first independent museum dedicated to Black culture, this Smithsonian-affiliated institution occupies 50,000 square feet within a historic park building. Its 15,000-piece collection features significant artifacts like Ida B. Wells's desk, Paul Laurence Dunbar's violin, and slavery-era relics alongside powerful contemporary art installations.

always_free_groups.category: Illinois residents who are Non-Active Duty Personnel or Prisoners of War
First Responders (Emergency Medical Technicians, Police, Firefighters, Coast Guard)
Museums for All (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program cardholders)
Bank of America employees and cardholders (first full weekend of each month)

free_days.eligibility: Up to 2 adults and 2 children via Explore More Illinois

Up to 2 adults and 2 children via Explore More Illinois
Up to 2 adults and 2 children via Explore More Illinois

name: Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum
description: This prominent local institution occupies a 73,000-square-foot facility featuring modern, landscape-integrated architecture designed to connect urban visitors with the natural world. It is celebrated for its world-class Judy Istock Butterfly Haven and extensive natural history collections focused on the unique ecology and wildlife of the Chicago region.

always_free_groups.category: Active-duty military personnel and their families
Electronic Benefits Transfer or Women Infants and Children cardholders ($1 admission)
Chicago Public Library Digital Pass holders

name: Driehaus Museum
description: A world-renowned decorative arts museum housed in a meticulously restored 24,000-square-foot Gilded Age mansion known as the Marble Palace. The collection features an extensive array of Tiffany glass, period furnishings, and 19th-century paintings set within opulent interiors of marble, onyx, and exotic woods.

always_free_groups.category: North American Reciprocal Museum Association Members
Reciprocal Organization of Associated Museums Members

name: Museum of Puerto Rican Arts
description: A unique cultural landmark and the only self-standing museum in the nation dedicated to Puerto Rican arts, this institution is housed in a historic Queen Anne revival-style former stable. It features three galleries and an extensive collection of over 300 traditional Vejigante masks and various diasporic artworks.

always_free_groups.category: National Museum of Puerto Rican Arts and Culture Members ($25-$1,000/year)

name: Intuit Center for Outsider Art
description: A premier Chicago institution and leading advocate for outsider art, this museum recently expanded to 18,000 square feet in the West Town neighborhood. It is renowned for its diverse collection of over 1,300 self-taught works, most notably the permanent Henry Darger Room Collection and the comprehensive Robert A. Roth Study Center.

always_free_groups.category: Youth and Young Adults 24 and under
Museums for All (Electronic Benefits Transfer or Women Infants and Children cardholders)
Reciprocal Organization of Associated Museums and North American Reciprocal Museum members
Visitors with disabilities and accompanying care partner

name: Loyola University Museum of Art
description: A 25,000-square-foot institution in a historic Gothic Revival building, this museum is a regional leader in exploring the intersection of art and spirituality. It is best known for the Martin D'Arcy, S.J. Collection, featuring over 300 exceptional medieval, Renaissance, and Baroque masterpieces and liturgical objects.

name: Museum of Contemporary Photography
description: A world-renowned tri-level institution, it is the premier college art museum and the only one in the Midwest dedicated exclusively to photography. Its vast collection of over 18,000 objects features masterpieces by influential photographers including Ansel Adams, Dorothea Lange, Harry Callahan, and Irving Penn.

name: Illinois Holocaust Museum
description: This world-renowned, 65,000-square-foot memorial is the second largest Holocaust museum in the United States, featuring a symbolic darkness to light architectural design. It houses poignant artifacts including an authentic early 20th-century German rail car and cutting-edge interactive holograms that allow visitors to converse with Holocaust survivors.

always_free_groups.category: Link and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program cardholders
North American Reciprocal Museum Association members
Illinois State Gold Star Identification cardholders
Explore More Illinois library pass holders
Bank of America cardholders (first full weekend of every month)
Medical and behavioral aides for special needs students

name: American Writers Museum
description: A unique, high-tech cultural destination, this 11,000-square-foot interactive space is the only museum in the nation dedicated to American literary heritage. It features immersive exhibits like the 60-foot American Voices timeline, the sensory-rich Surprise Bookshelf, the mesmerizing Word Waterfall, and a popular collection of functional vintage typewriters.

always_free_groups.category: Active-duty United States military personnel and their families
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program Electronic Benefits Transfer cardholders
Participating Illinois public library cardholders via Explore More Illinois
Illinois library cardholders via Museum Adventure Pass
Reciprocal Organization of Associated Museums members
North American Reciprocal Museum Association members

name: Chicago Cultural Center
description: A landmark Beaux-Arts building and cultural institution, the former Chicago Public Library now serves as the city's premier free arts venue. It features two magnificent Tiffany stained-glass domes, 34,000 square feet of exhibition space, and hosts hundreds of free concerts, exhibitions, films, and lectures each year.

name: Poetry Foundation
description: An independent literary organization and publisher based in a modernist, silver-LEED certified building in Chicago. This prominent institution features a 30,000-volume library, a performance space, and an extensive digital archive housing iconic works by poets like T.S. Eliot, Langston Hughes, and Gwendolyn Brooks.

name: Chinese American Museum
description: Housed in the historic former Quong Yick Co. building, this local cultural institution serves as a vital hub for preserving the history and culture of Chinese Americans in the Midwest. The museum features rotating exhibitions of regional art and innovation that spotlight the unique narratives and community contributions of the diaspora.

name: Polish Museum of America
description: One of the oldest and largest ethnic museums in the United States, this institution is housed in a three-story Classical Revival building in Chicago. It features a world-class collection including a massive 1939 World's Fair stained-glass window, the Royal Sleigh, and the extensive Ignacy Jan Paderewski memorabilia.

always_free_groups.category: Polish Museum of America Sustaining Members
Polish Museum of America Life Members

name: Swedish American Museum
description: A 24,000-square-foot heritage museum in Chicago's Andersonville, serving as a cultural anchor for the Midwestern Nordic community. Its three-story facility features two gallery spaces and a children's museum, housing 12,000 objects including a permanent immigration exhibit, Anders Zorn etchings, and personal letters from Swedish immigrants.

always_free_groups.category: Children under the age of one

name: National Hellenic Museum
description: A prominent public history museum in Chicago's Greektown, this 40,000-square-foot, four-story facility features a modern design inspired by Greek monasteries. It houses over 20,000 artifacts, including ancient coins, traditional textiles, and extensive oral histories that preserve the legacy of the Greek American immigrant experience.

always_free_groups.category: American Association for State and Local History members

name: Irish American Heritage Center
address: 4626 N Knox Ave, Chicago, IL  Irish American Heritage Center Members ($25-$1,000/year)
description: A local cultural hub and museum located in a sprawling four-story, former Chicago school building covering an entire city block. It features historic architecture, Celtic art, and a vast collection including Belleek Parian China, Irish lace, and a tapestry by Lily Yeats.

name: International Museum of Surgical Science
address: 1524 N Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60610
description: North America's only museum dedicated to surgery is housed in a historic four-story Beaux-Arts mansion featuring 10,000 square feet of gallery space. The collection includes over 7,000 medical artifacts, such as the Lindbergh perfusion pump, ancient trephined skulls, an iron lung, and the iconic Hall of Immortals statues.

always_free_groups.category: International Museum of Surgical Science Members (~$45-100/year)
Association of Science and Technology Centers Members
Electronic Benefits Transfer cardholders (Museums for All)

name: Chicago Maritime Museum
description: Located in the Bridgeport Art Center, this 10,000-square-foot museum is a local authority on the region's waterways and maritime traditions. The sleek, modern facility features nine permanent exhibitions showcasing artifacts like the Ralph and Rita Frese Canoe Collection and a dive suit from the Eastland disaster.

name: Pritzker Military Museum
description: This non-partisan research library and museum, housed in an award-winning modern architectural masterpiece on 280 acres, holds a distinguished national reputation for military history. Its expansive collection of over 115,000 items features rare 17th-century manuscripts, significant Winston Churchill materials, and a profound, permanent Medal of Honor exhibit.

always_free_groups.category: Pritzker Military Museum and Library Members ($60 - $500+/year)
Active Duty Military, Retired Military, and Veterans

name: Museum of Broadcast Communications
description: One of only three U.S. institutions dedicated to media history, this nonprofit currently operates a Chicago pop-up location featuring extensive archives and interactive exhibits. It showcases iconic artifacts like Johnny Carson’s set, Saturday Night Live costumes, and the Radio Hall of Fame, preserving the legacy of American radio and television.

always_free_groups.category: Paid personal care assistants accompanying a visitor with a disability

name: Busy Beaver Button Museum
description: The world's only museum dedicated to pin-back buttons, this unique archive is located inside a vibrant Chicago production facility. It houses a massive collection of over 30,000 pieces, most notably featuring rare George Washington inauguration buttons and historic Abraham Lincoln campaign pins.

name: Leather Archives & Museum
description: This world-renowned institution is the only museum globally dedicated to leather and fetish history, spanning 10,000 square feet across two floors in a former Chicago synagogue. Its extensive collection features iconic erotic murals by Etienne, historical motorcycle club patches, and a library of over 5,000 books documenting LGBTQ+ subcultures.

name: Bronzeville Children's Museum
address: 9301 S Stony Island Ave, Chicago, IL 60617
description: The first and only African American children's museum in the country, this institution features interactive guided tours within its expansive South Side facility. It highlights cultural heritage through prominent exhibits like the Motherland and the Bronzeville Gallery, showcasing historical contributions from figures such as Jean Baptist DuSable and Lewis Latimer.

name: Chicago Children's Museum
description: A national leader in play-based learning located at Navy Pier, this institution spans 84,000 square feet across three floors of interactive exhibits. It features prominent attractions like the Cloud Buster climbing sculpture, the Kovler Family Climbing Schooner, and the Dinosaur Expedition where children can dig for fossils.

always_free_groups.category: First 250 registered children and families with disabilities for Play for All events

free_days.eligibility: All children (must be accompanied by an adult)

name: Brookfield Zoo
description: A world-renowned 235-acre nature park, this institution is a pioneer in using naturalistic moats instead of traditional cages. It houses over 3,400 animals across 511 species, featuring immersive attractions like the massive Tropic World indoor rainforest and the Great Bear Wilderness, which showcases polar bears and Mexican gray wolves.

name: Chicago Botanic Garden
description: A world-renowned living museum and conservation science center spanning 385 acres across nine islands. This preeminent botanical destination features 27 display gardens and 2.5 million plants, including a celebrated bonsai collection, an English walled garden, and the LEED Gold-rated Daniel F. and Ada L. Rice Plant Conservation Science Center.

always_free_groups.category: United States military reservists and former prisoners of war
Illinois Link and Women Infants and Children cardholders
Chicago Public Library Kids Museum Passport holders
American Horticultural Society Reciprocal Admissions Program members

name: Morton Arboretum
description: An internationally recognized outdoor museum and tree research center spanning 1,700 acres of diverse woodlands, prairies, and lakes. This scientific institution features living collections of over 200,000 cataloged plants, including a 4-acre interactive children's garden, a one-acre maze, and more than 4,100 different plant species.

always_free_groups.category: Active-duty military members and their families
Members of American Horticultural Society Reciprocal Garden Network

name: Lincoln Park Conservatory
description: This historic Victorian Era glass house is a three-acre botanical landmark featuring four lush display houses showcasing exotic flora from around the globe. It is a nationally renowned destination famous for its extensive collections of tropical palms, ancient cycads, and rare orchids, providing a serene tropical escape.

name: Chicago Architecture Center
description: An acclaimed nonprofit cultural institution located in a landmark Mies van der Rohe building, featuring nearly 10,000 square feet of riverfront exhibition space. It is renowned for its immersive architectural displays, including a massive 3D-printed scale model of Chicago and super-sized models of the world's tallest skyscrapers.

name: Robie House
description: A world-renowned historic house museum and National Historic Landmark, this three-story, 9,065-square-foot residence is the consummate expression of Frank Lloyd Wright's Prairie style. It is most famous for its dramatic cantilevered rooflines, open floor plan, and 174 exquisite art glass windows and doors that seamlessly blend interior and exterior spaces.

name: Ukrainian Institute of Modern Art
description: A nationally and internationally acclaimed modern art museum located in a 2,100 square foot gallery in Chicago's West Town. It features one of the world's largest collections of Ukrainian-American abstract and minimalist works, including notable pieces by Alexander Archipenko, Alexis Gritchenko, and Richard Hunt.

always_free_groups.category: Ukrainian Institute of Modern Art Members ($30+/year)

name: DePaul Art Museum
description: A world-class contemporary art museum located in a 15,350-square-foot, three-story LEED-certified building in Lincoln Park. Its permanent collection of over 4,000 objects features international modern art, Chicago Imagists, and works by notable artists like Andy Warhol, Christina Ramberg, and Martin Puryear.

name: Wrightwood 659
description: A private exhibition space in Chicago dedicated to architecture and socially engaged art, designed by Pritzker Prize-winner Tadao Ando. This 38,000-square-foot contemporary gallery features a stunning three-story brick atrium and hosts rotating exhibitions from artists like Ai Weiwei and Martin Wong within its minimalist concrete and steel interior.

name: Design Museum of Chicago
description: A local non-profit institution located in a single-floor gallery within the Expo 72 building, focusing on diverse design disciplines from architecture to graphic arts. It features rotating, community-based exhibitions with notable items like the Letters Beyond Form typographic collection and archival pieces from the Great Ideas of Humanity series.

name: Glessner House
description: An internationally known architectural treasure, this 17,000-square-foot National Historic Landmark is a masterpiece of the Richardsonian Romanesque style. It houses a premier collection of original Arts and Crafts decorative objects and furniture by renowned designers such as William Morris, William De Morgan, and Isaac Scott.

always_free_groups.category: National Trust for Historic Preservation Members ($15/year)

name: Hull-House Museum
description: A world-renowned National Historic Landmark and museum consisting of two original Italianate buildings on a one-acre site. It preserves the legacy of social reform through exhibits featuring Jane Addams’ 1931 Nobel Peace Prize medal and her extensive FBI file.

always_free_groups.category: University of Illinois Chicago faculty, staff, and students
Chicago Public Schools Title I schools (with documentation)

name: Pullman National Park
address: 11141 S Cottage Grove Ave, Chicago, IL 60628
description: A nationally significant historic district and Chicago's first National Park Service unit, this 300-acre site preserves America's first planned industrial community. It features landmark Queen Anne and Romanesque buildings, including the restored Administration Clock Tower and Hotel Florence, which highlight the site's pivotal role in U.S. labor and civil rights history.

name: Pullman Porter Museum
description: A nationally recognized institution and the only one of its kind, this museum is located in a historic building within Chicago's Pullman National Historical Park. It features a permanent collection of African American labor history artifacts and a 10-foot outdoor mosaic sculpture dedicated to the Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters.

name: Oriental Institute (ISAC)
description: A world-renowned research center and museum housed in a Gothic Revival building, featuring over 350,000 artifacts from West Asia and North Africa. It is famous for prominent pieces such as a 40-ton winged bull from Khorsabad, a 16-foot statue of King Tutankhamun, and the Megiddo Ivories.

always_free_groups.category: University of Chicago students, faculty, and staff
Institute for the Study of Ancient Cultures Members ($40-$50/year)
